description: I/O contracts for all dbSherpa node types. All datasets are pandas DataFrames passed by name through the shared RunContext.

ALERT_TRIGGER.description: Entry point — binds alert payload to context

inputs.alert_payload: JSON object passed at workflow invocation time.

outputs.context_keys: One context key per declared alert_field, e.g. trader_id, book, alert_date, currency_pair, alert_id.

config_schema.alert_fields: Map of field_name → type (string|date|number). Non-listed payload keys in extras.standard_alert_fields are still bound when present in the alert.

constraints: Must be the first node (id=n01).
No dataset inputs or outputs.

inputs.name: alert_payload
inputs.description: JSON object passed at workflow invocation time.

outputs.name: context_keys
outputs.description: One context key per declared alert_field, e.g. trader_id, book, alert_date, currency_pair, alert_id.

params.name: alert_fields
params.description: Map of field_name → type (string|date|number). Non-listed payload keys in extras.standard_alert_fields are still bound when present in the alert.

COMMS_COLLECTOR.description: Query Oculus comms with keyword scanning

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.comms: DataFrame with columns: user, timestamp, display_post, event_type, _keyword_hit, _matched_keywords. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].
outputs.keyword_hit_count: Total keyword hit count (int). Stored as {output_name}_keyword_hits.

config_schema.mock_csv_path: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

constraints: Always adds _keyword_hit (boolean) and _matched_keywords (list[str]) columns.
Scans display_post field only.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.name: comms
outputs.description: DataFrame with columns: user, timestamp, display_post, event_type, _keyword_hit, _matched_keywords. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].

outputs.name: keyword_hit_count
outputs.description: Total keyword hit count (int). Stored as {output_name}_keyword_hits.

params.name: mock_csv_path
params.description: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

CONSOLIDATED_SUMMARY.description: LLM executive summary across all sections

inputs.sections: All section objects produced by upstream SECTION_SUMMARY nodes (context.sections).

outputs.executive_summary: Multi-paragraph executive summary. Stored as context.executive_summary.

config_schema.llm_prompt_template: Custom prompt with {section_text}, {trader_id}, {currency_pair}, {disposition}, {flag_count} placeholders. Falls back to the built-in template when empty.

constraints: LLM model: claude-sonnet-4-6, max_tokens: 1000.
Must run after all SECTION_SUMMARY nodes.

inputs.name: sections
inputs.description: All section objects produced by upstream SECTION_SUMMARY nodes (context.sections).

outputs.name: executive_summary
outputs.description: Multi-paragraph executive summary. Stored as context.executive_summary.

params.name: llm_prompt_template
params.description: Custom prompt with {section_text}, {trader_id}, {currency_pair}, {disposition}, {flag_count} placeholders. Falls back to the built-in template when empty.

DATA_HIGHLIGHTER.description: Apply colour rules to dataset rows

config_schema.rules: Array of {condition: string (pandas eval expression), colour: string (hex #RRGGBB), label: string}.

constraints: Conditions are evaluated with pandas DataFrame.eval().
Rules are applied in order — last matching rule wins.
Rows with no matching rule get colour #FFFFFF and empty label.

params.name: rules
params.description: Array of {condition: string (pandas eval expression), colour: string (hex #RRGGBB), label: string}.

inputs.flag_count: Flag count from SIGNAL_CALCULATOR (read from ctx.values[{input_name}_flag_count] if the dataset isn't available).

outputs.flag_count: Total signal hits (int). Stored as context.flag_count.
outputs.output_branch: Branch name to route to. Stored as context.output_branch.

config_schema.flag_count_expr: Python expression using 'flag_count' variable, e.g. 'flag_count > 0'. Overrides escalate/review thresholds when supplied.

inputs.name: flag_count
inputs.description: Flag count from SIGNAL_CALCULATOR (read from ctx.values[{input_name}_flag_count] if the dataset isn't available).

outputs.name: flag_count
outputs.description: Total signal hits (int). Stored as context.flag_count.

outputs.name: output_branch
outputs.description: Branch name to route to. Stored as context.output_branch.

params.name: flag_count_expr
params.description: Python expression using 'flag_count' variable, e.g. 'flag_count > 0'. Overrides escalate/review thresholds when supplied.

EXECUTION_DATA_COLLECTOR.description: Query Solr for client orders, executions, trades, and quotes

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}

outputs.executions: Order/execution rows. Stored in ctx.datasets under the configured output_name.
outputs.row_count: Integer row count. Stored in ctx.values as {output_name}_count.

config_schema.query_template: Solr query; use {context.xxx} placeholders for alert fields.
config_schema.loop_over_books: Repeat the query for each book in the books list.
config_schema.mock_csv_path: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing. CSV columns are not re-checked against the lists below; synthetic rows are.

constraints: When source reads versioned trades/fills, trade_version:1 MUST be hard-coded in query_template — never from context.
Output DataFrame will always include trade_version=1 for hs_execution, hs_trades, and hs_orders_and_executions synthetic reads.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}

outputs.name: executions
outputs.description: Order/execution rows. Stored in ctx.datasets under the configured output_name.

outputs.name: row_count
outputs.description: Integer row count. Stored in ctx.values as {output_name}_count.

params.name: query_template
params.description: Solr query; use {context.xxx} placeholders for alert fields.

params.name: loop_over_books
params.description: Repeat the query for each book in the books list.

params.name: mock_csv_path
params.description: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing. CSV columns are not re-checked against the lists below; synthetic rows are.

FEATURE_ENGINE.description: Apply ordered feature operations to a dataset

outputs.features: Input DataFrame plus columns added or renamed by configured operations.

config_schema.operations: Ordered operations. Supported op values: rename, lifecycle_event, derive_signed_notional.

constraints: rename uses {op:'rename', columns:{old_col:new_col}}.
lifecycle_event adds _prev_status, _status_changed, _lifecycle_event when id/status columns exist.
derive_signed_notional adds signed_notional using quantity/price/side columns.

outputs.name: features
outputs.description: Input DataFrame plus columns added or renamed by configured operations.

params.name: operations
params.description: Ordered operations. Supported op values: rename, lifecycle_event, derive_signed_notional.

MARKET_DATA_COLLECTOR.description: Query EBS/Mercury tick data, normalise timestamps

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.ticks: DataFrame with columns: timestamp (ISO str), symbol (str), bid, ask, mid, spread_pips, bid_size, ask_size, venue_name, seq_no. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].

config_schema.mock_csv_path: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

constraints: Normalises raw_timestamp (nanosecond int) → ISO-8601 string.
Normalises byte-string fields (raw_symbol, venue) → plain str.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}.

outputs.name: ticks
outputs.description: DataFrame with columns: timestamp (ISO str), symbol (str), bid, ask, mid, spread_pips, bid_size, ask_size, venue_name, seq_no. Stored under ctx.datasets[output_name].

params.name: mock_csv_path
params.description: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator. Ignored if the file is missing.

ORACLE_DATA_COLLECTOR.description: Query Oracle surveillance warehouse order/execution extracts

inputs.context: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}

outputs.rows: Oracle extract rows. Stored in ctx.datasets under the configured output_name.
outputs.row_count: Integer row count. Stored in ctx.values as {output_name}_count.

config_schema.query_template: Oracle SQL template; use {context.xxx} placeholders for alert fields.
config_schema.mock_csv_path: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: Context keys referenced in query_template as {context.xxx}

outputs.name: rows
outputs.description: Oracle extract rows. Stored in ctx.datasets under the configured output_name.

outputs.name: row_count
outputs.description: Integer row count. Stored in ctx.values as {output_name}_count.

params.name: query_template
params.description: Oracle SQL template; use {context.xxx} placeholders for alert fields.

params.name: mock_csv_path
params.description: Demo-mode override: path to a CSV used verbatim instead of the synthetic generator.

REPORT_OUTPUT.description: Generate Excel report with tabs & highlights

inputs.datasets: All DataFrames to include as tabs (ctx.datasets).
inputs.sections: Section narratives for the Section Summaries sheet.
inputs.context: disposition, trader_id, currency_pair etc. used on the cover page.

outputs.report_path: Absolute path to the written .xlsx file. Stored as context.report_path.

config_schema.output_path: File path for the Excel output (e.g. 'output/report.xlsx').
config_schema.tabs: Array of {name: string (max 31 chars), dataset: string, include_highlights: boolean}. When empty, all context.datasets are included.

constraints: Tab names truncated to 31 characters (Excel limit).
Datetime columns converted to strings automatically.
List/dict cell values stringified automatically.
If include_highlights=true, uses dataset_name + '_highlighted' if it exists.
Must be the final node in the workflow.

inputs.name: datasets
inputs.description: All DataFrames to include as tabs (ctx.datasets).

inputs.name: sections
inputs.description: Section narratives for the Section Summaries sheet.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: disposition, trader_id, currency_pair etc. used on the cover page.

outputs.name: report_path
outputs.description: Absolute path to the written .xlsx file. Stored as context.report_path.

params.name: output_path
params.description: File path for the Excel output (e.g. 'output/report.xlsx').

params.name: tabs
params.description: Array of {name: string (max 31 chars), dataset: string, include_highlights: boolean}. When empty, all context.datasets are included.

inputs.context: trader_id, currency_pair, disposition consumed by the prompt template.

outputs.section: {name, stats, narrative, dataset}. Stored under context.sections[section_name].

constraints: LLM model: claude-sonnet-4-6, max_tokens: 600.

inputs.name: context
inputs.description: trader_id, currency_pair, disposition consumed by the prompt template.

outputs.name: section
outputs.description: {name, stats, narrative, dataset}. Stored under context.sections[section_name].

outputs.flag_count: Number of rows where _signal_flag == True. Stored as {output_name}_flag_count.

config_schema.script_path: Path to custom Python script (upload_script mode).
config_schema.script_content: Inline Python snippet operating on local variable `df` (upload_script mode).

constraints: ALWAYS outputs exactly these 5 columns: _signal_flag, _signal_score, _signal_reason, _signal_type, _signal_window.
Missing signal columns are auto-filled with defaults (False, 0.0, '', '', '').
Custom scripts must operate on local variable 'df' and leave result in 'df'.

outputs.name: flag_count
outputs.description: Number of rows where _signal_flag == True. Stored as {output_name}_flag_count.

params.name: script_path
params.description: Path to custom Python script (upload_script mode).

params.name: script_content
params.description: Inline Python snippet operating on local variable `df` (upload_script mode).
